texto: What are you - Secret Service?''If I were, I wouldn't admit it.''And you're not admitting it, I notice.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robert Goddard que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “What are you - Secret Service?''If I were, I wouldn't admit it.''And you're not admitting it, I n…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, mind-games, witty, com foco em witty e witty-comebacks.

texto: Photographs don’t discriminate between the living and the dead. In the fragments of time and shards of light that compose them, everyone is equal. Now you see us; now you don’t. It doesn’t matter whether you look through a camera lens and press the shutter. It doesn’t even matter whether you open your eyes or close them. The pictures are always there. And so are the people in them.
explicacao: Citação de Robert Goddard sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Photographs don’t discriminate between the living and the dead. In the fragments of time and shar…” articula vida, light, photography em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como photography e time.

frase_original: Photographs don’t discriminate between the living and the dead. In the fragments of time and shards of light that compose them, everyone is equal. Now you see us; now you don’t. It doesn’t matter whether you look through a camera lens and press the shutter. It doesn’t even matter whether you open your eyes or close them. The pictures are always there. And so are the people in them.

texto: In a world that thought itself so wise yet behaved so stupidly, it was possible sometimes to believe that only the mad saw matters as they truly were, that only people like my brother were prepared to admit what they saw from the corner of their eye.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robert Goddard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “In a world that thought itself so wise yet behaved so stupidly, it was possible sometimes to beli…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, madness, sanity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de sanity.
